_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Enter work into Task and project management tools used for software development (e.g. Jira, Confluence, Trac, MediaWiki, etc.)
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of software version control concepts and Git workflows, including branching strategies, merge and rebase operations, conflict resolution, pull request processes, and collaborative development practices.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-K-008 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-S-011 (TF, cosine: 0.510), TF-S-004 (TF, cosine: 0.509).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.0/10 (raw 0.400). Shared vocabulary: [development, software]. DCWF-only terms [confluence, enter, etc, jira, management, mediawiki] vs WIDAI-only terms [branching, collaborative, concepts, conflict, control, git] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.848 (P=0.849, R=0.847). Balanced P/R — the 18-word DCWF element and 25-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.4%). "Enter work into Task and project management tools used for software development" and "Knowledge of software version control concepts and Git workflows, including bran" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.527 > STS 0.400 (gap +0.127). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [development, software] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.7%. Shared tokens: [development, software]. DCWF-only: [confluence, enter, etc, jira, management, mediawiki]. WIDAI-only: [branching, collaborative, concepts, conflict, control, git].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.127 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
